name: Caliper 1.2 Event Dictionary
plain_description: A structured dictionary for the platform's Caliper event ingestion and projection model. It describes Sensor API envelopes, immutable events, indexed entities, actors, context, profile rules, and governed extensions exposed as SQL and JSON.
model_scope: Event ingestion metadata, immutable raw event retention, search projections, profile/action vocabulary, entity indexing, LTI context joins, and privacy-governed extensions. This is not a formal Caliper certification claim.

objects.name: Caliper Envelope
objects.plain_description: One Sensor API payload received from a tool, platform, or app.
objects.why_it_exists: The envelope preserves source, send time, version, validation status, and raw payload lineage before individual events are projected.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this received Caliper envelope.
The school, district, or customer boundary that owns this event payload.
The tool, platform, app, or sensor that sent the payload.
When the sensor says it sent the payload.
The Caliper context or version used to interpret the payload.
How many event objects were found in the payload.
When the platform received the payload.
Whether the payload was accepted, rejected, or held for review.
The original JSON-LD payload retained for audit, replay, and conformance review.

objects.name: Caliper Event
objects.plain_description: One immutable learning activity event projected from a Caliper envelope.
objects.why_it_exists: Events are the core behavioral history for learning analytics, audit, and cross-standard joins without making analytics the gradebook source of truth.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this projected event row.
The received Caliper envelope that contained this event.
The globally unique event identifier supplied by the sender.
The Caliper event class, such as AssessmentEvent, MediaEvent, or ToolLaunchEvent.
The Caliper profile that explains which event, action, actor, object, and generated values are expected.
What happened in the event.
The indexed person, app, or agent that performed the action.
The indexed entity that the actor acted on.
When the learning activity happened.
The indexed application where the event happened.
The indexed entity created or produced by the event, such as an attempt, response, score, annotation, or search response.
A specific location or target inside the object, such as a media location, page, link, or question target.
The prior app, page, resource, or context that led to this event.
The class, course, group, or organization context for the event.
The actor's role and status relationship in the group context.
The app or user session that groups related events.
The LTI or federated session context connected to a tool launch.
The original event JSON-LD object retained for audit, replay, and deeper profile-specific projection.

objects.name: Caliper Entity
objects.plain_description: A normalized index entry for a Caliper person, app, resource, attempt, score, session, or other event object.
objects.why_it_exists: Entity indexing lets the platform search event objects, join to roster/content systems, and avoid flattening every Caliper type into a separate table at first.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this indexed entity.
The Caliper IRI or source identifier for the entity.
The Caliper entity class, such as Person, Assessment, Score, Session, or DigitalResource.
A human-readable name for the entity when one is supplied.
A short explanation or label for the entity.
When the source says this entity was created.
When the source says this entity last changed.
Other identifiers known for this entity, such as SIS, LTI, OneRoster, or internal IDs.
Governed source-specific fields carried on the entity.
The platform person ID when policy and identifiers allow this entity to resolve to a known person.
The platform resource, QTI, CASE, or content ID when this entity resolves to known learning content.

objects.name: Caliper Actor
objects.plain_description: The person, application, group, or agent that performs a Caliper event action.
objects.why_it_exists: Actors need explicit resolution rules because raw Caliper actors may be known roster users, apps, groups, or unresolved source agents.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this actor projection.
The indexed Caliper entity that represents the actor.
Whether the actor is a person, software app, group, or generic agent.
The resolved platform person ID when the actor is a known school user.
A display name supplied for the actor or resolved from a trusted source.
The kind of source identifier used to resolve the actor.
The source identifier value used for matching or lineage.
Whether the actor was matched to a canonical platform record.

objects.name: Caliper Context
objects.plain_description: The group, membership, session, and LTI context surrounding a Caliper event.
objects.why_it_exists: Context connects activity to classes, roles, sessions, and tool launches without losing the original event envelope.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this event context projection.
The Caliper event this context belongs to.
The Caliper group, course section, organization, or class context entity.
The Caliper membership entity that describes the actor's relationship to the group.
The LIS role supplied in the Caliper membership context.
Whether the membership is active or inactive in the source context.
The Caliper session entity that groups related activity.
The LTI or federated session entity connected to the event.
The LTI launch message type when the event came from an LTI workflow.
The LTI platform issuer or platform identifier tied to the federated session.
The LTI deployment identifier that scopes the tool relationship.
The LTI course or context identifier supplied by the platform.

objects.name: Caliper Profile Rule
objects.plain_description: A governed row describing an expected Caliper profile, event, action, actor, object, and generated or target combination.
objects.why_it_exists: Profile rules give developers and validators a machine-readable crosswalk from Caliper profiles to the event/action/entity combinations the platform expects.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this profile rule.
The Caliper profile the rule belongs to.
The Caliper event class allowed by the profile rule.
The expected actor class for the profile rule.
One action allowed for this event/profile combination.
The expected object entity type for the rule.
The generated entity, target entity, or narrative condition expected by the rule.
How the platform should validate, project, or join events matching this rule.
fields.school_example: Link Assessment objects to QTI tests when identifiers match.

objects.name: Caliper Extension
objects.plain_description: A governed custom field found under a Caliper extensions object.
objects.why_it_exists: Extensions can carry useful vendor data, but they need namespace, privacy, and policy controls before becoming queryable platform fields.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this extension projection.
Whether this extension belongs to an envelope, event, entity, actor, or context row.
The platform row ID that owns this extension value.
The governed namespace or vendor domain for the extension key.
The extension field name within the namespace.
The normalized scalar value when the extension can be safely indexed.
The platform privacy classification assigned to this extension value.
Whether tenant policy allows this extension to be retained and queried.

data_version.label: Caliper 1.2 context
data_version.plain_description: The JSON-LD context IRI used for Caliper 1.2 payloads.

ingestion_status.label: Received
ingestion_status.plain_description: The payload was received but has not completed validation.

ingestion_status.label: Valid
ingestion_status.plain_description: The payload passed validation for the platform's current projection.

ingestion_status.label: Rejected
ingestion_status.plain_description: The payload failed validation and should not be projected.

ingestion_status.label: Quarantined
ingestion_status.plain_description: The payload is held for review because policy, privacy, or schema checks need attention.

event_type.label: Annotation event
event_type.plain_description: A person creates or shares an annotation on a digital resource.

event_type.label: Assessment event
event_type.plain_description: A person works with a whole assessment.

event_type.label: Assessment item event
event_type.plain_description: A person works with one assessment item or question.

event_type.label: Assignable event
event_type.plain_description: A person works with assigned digital content.

event_type.label: Feedback event
event_type.plain_description: A person comments on or rates something.

event_type.label: Forum event
event_type.plain_description: A person subscribes or unsubscribes from a forum.

event_type.label: Grade event
event_type.plain_description: A person or app grades an attempt.

event_type.label: Media event
event_type.plain_description: A person interacts with audio, video, or media.

event_type.label: Message event
event_type.plain_description: A person posts or marks a message.

event_type.label: Navigation event
event_type.plain_description: A person navigates to a resource, app, or location.

event_type.label: Questionnaire event
event_type.plain_description: A person starts or submits a questionnaire.

event_type.label: Questionnaire item event
event_type.plain_description: A person works with one questionnaire item.

event_type.label: Resource management event
event_type.plain_description: A person manages a digital resource.

event_type.label: Search event
event_type.plain_description: A person searches a resource or app.

event_type.label: Session event
event_type.plain_description: A person or app starts, ends, or times out a session.

event_type.label: Survey event
event_type.plain_description: A person opts into or out of a survey.

event_type.label: Survey invitation event
event_type.plain_description: A person sends or responds to a survey invitation.

event_type.label: Thread event
event_type.plain_description: A person marks a forum thread read or unread.

event_type.label: Tool launch event
event_type.plain_description: A person launches or returns from a tool.

event_type.label: Tool use event
event_type.plain_description: A person uses a software application.

event_type.label: View event
event_type.plain_description: A person views something.

profile.label: General profile
profile.plain_description: Generic profile for events not covered by a narrower profile.

profile.label: Annotation profile
profile.plain_description: Annotation, bookmark, highlight, share, and tag activity.

profile.label: Reading profile
profile.plain_description: Reading, navigation, and viewing resource activity.

profile.label: Resource management profile
profile.plain_description: Resource creation, upload, publish, print, download, and similar management activity.

profile.label: Session profile
profile.plain_description: Login, logout, and timeout session activity.

profile.label: Tool launch profile
profile.plain_description: LTI or tool launch and return activity.

action.label: Abandoned
action.plain_description: A user abandoned something before completion.

action.label: Accepted
action.plain_description: A user accepted an invitation or request.

action.label: Attached
action.plain_description: Something was attached to another thing.

action.label: Closed popout
action.plain_description: A media popout or window was closed.

action.label: Completed
action.plain_description: A user completed an item or task.

action.label: Created
action.plain_description: A resource or entity was created.

action.label: Declined
action.plain_description: A user declined an invitation or request.

action.label: Deleted
action.plain_description: A resource or entity was deleted.

action.label: Described
action.plain_description: Metadata or description was added or changed.

action.label: Highlighted
action.plain_description: Text or resource content was highlighted.

action.label: Jumped to
action.plain_description: Media or navigation jumped to a location.

action.label: Launched
action.plain_description: A tool or app was launched.

action.label: Marked as read
action.plain_description: A message or thread was marked read.

action.label: Marked as unread
action.plain_description: A message or thread was marked unread.

action.label: Modified
action.plain_description: A resource or entity was changed.

action.label: Navigated to
action.plain_description: A user navigated to a resource or location.

action.label: Opened popout
action.plain_description: A media popout or window was opened.

action.label: Ranked
action.plain_description: A user rated or ranked something.

action.label: Reset
action.plain_description: Activity or an attempt was reset.

action.label: Returned
action.plain_description: A user returned from a launched tool or workflow.

action.label: Saved
action.plain_description: A resource or work item was saved.

action.label: Sent
action.plain_description: A message or invitation was sent.

action.label: Skipped
action.plain_description: A learner skipped an item or task.

action.label: Started
action.plain_description: A learner or user started something.

action.label: Submitted
action.plain_description: Work or an attempt was submitted.

action.label: Uploaded
action.plain_description: A resource or file was uploaded.

action.label: Used
action.plain_description: A user used a tool or application.

entity_type.label: Aggregate measure
entity_type.plain_description: A computed usage or learning metric.

entity_type.label: Annotation
entity_type.plain_description: A note or mark connected to a digital resource.

entity_type.label: Assignable digital resource
entity_type.plain_description: Digital content assigned to a learner.

entity_type.label: Attempt
entity_type.plain_description: One try at an assignment, assessment, or questionnaire.

entity_type.label: Date/time question
entity_type.plain_description: Question asking for a date or time.

entity_type.label: Frame
entity_type.plain_description: Segment or location in a resource.

entity_type.label: Learning objective
entity_type.plain_description: Learning goal or objective, preferably linked to CASE when available.

entity_type.label: Membership
entity_type.plain_description: A person's relationship to a group or organization.

entity_type.label: Organization
entity_type.plain_description: School, district, provider, or other organization.

entity_type.label: Text position selector
entity_type.plain_description: Text-range selector without an entity ID.

entity_type.label: System identifier
entity_type.plain_description: Other system identifier without an entity ID.

actor_type.label: Agent
actor_type.plain_description: A generic person or software agent.

actor_type.label: Software application
actor_type.plain_description: An app or tool acting as the event actor.

actor_type.label: Group
actor_type.plain_description: A group acting as or standing in for the actor.

resolution_status.label: Resolved
resolution_status.plain_description: The actor or entity was matched to a canonical platform record.

resolution_status.label: Unresolved
resolution_status.plain_description: The actor or entity remains source-only until a safe match is available.

resolution_status.label: Conflict
resolution_status.plain_description: The source identifiers matched multiple possible platform records.

resolution_status.label: Blocked by policy
resolution_status.plain_description: Privacy or tenant policy prevents canonical matching.

lis_status.label: Active
lis_status.plain_description: The membership or status is active.

lis_status.label: Inactive
lis_status.plain_description: The membership or status is inactive.

extension_owner_type.label: Envelope
extension_owner_type.plain_description: The extension belongs to a Caliper envelope.

extension_owner_type.label: Event
extension_owner_type.plain_description: The extension belongs to a Caliper event.

extension_owner_type.label: Entity
extension_owner_type.plain_description: The extension belongs to a Caliper entity.

extension_owner_type.label: Actor
extension_owner_type.plain_description: The extension belongs to a Caliper actor projection.

extension_owner_type.label: Context
extension_owner_type.plain_description: The extension belongs to a Caliper context projection.

extension_privacy.label: Public
extension_privacy.plain_description: The extension can be treated as public metadata.

extension_privacy.label: Education record
extension_privacy.plain_description: The extension may identify or describe a student's education record.

extension_privacy.label: Behavioral
extension_privacy.plain_description: The extension describes learning behavior or activity.

extension_privacy.label: Restricted
extension_privacy.plain_description: The extension is sensitive and requires explicit policy before use.

unsupported_or_deferred.area: Full Sensor API validation, immutable storage, and replay/idempotency handling
unsupported_or_deferred.reason: A minimal authenticated receipt Edge Function exists. Full Sensor API conformance still needs profile validation, immutable event storage, replay handling, retention policy, and operational controls.
The first generated slice indexes core event/entity fields and preserves raw JSON-LD while profile-specific projections are added incrementally based on product value.
Requires retention, aggregation, access control, and de-identification decisions beyond the event receipt path.
Requires conformance testing and organizational certification work beyond these generated dictionary artifacts.
